items.question: Why are React state updates considered asynchronous?
items.answer: React batches state updates and applies them before the next render for performance. So reading the state variable right after calling the setter still gives the old value within the same event handler. Use the functional updater or useEffect to react to the new value.

items.question: What happens if you mutate state directly?
items.answer: React compares state by reference. Mutating the same object/array doesn't change its reference, so React may skip the re-render and the UI goes stale. Always create a new value.
